summary: Re-enabled the C object for sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c as matching and removed the fallback assembly file asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.s. The build lists were updated so gobjgxlink links from src rather than asm, converting this baselib GX-link module from assembly-backed to C-backed in the normal build.

key_files.role: Deleted assembly fallback for the gobjgxlink module; the removed asm contained GObj_GXReorder, GObj_SetupGXLink, GObj_SetupGXLinkMax, GObj_SetupGXLinkMaxSorted, HSD_GObjGXLink_8039084C, HSD_GObjGXLink_80390908, HSD_GObjGXLink_803909D8, and local assert string/data objects.
Changed sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...), re-enabling matching C linkage for the generated build configuration.
Swapped TEXT_O_FILES entry from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.s.o to src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c.o.

what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.s, removing 363 lines of assembly and associated .data labels for this module.
Marked sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c as Matching in configure.py instead of NonMatching.
Updated obj_files.mk so the linked object is $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c.o rather than $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.s.o.

The deleted assembly shows the module covered GX link list insertion, removal, reordering, max-link setup, sorted insertion, and assert checks against HSD_GObjLibInitData.gx_link_max and HSD_GOBJ_GXLINK_NONE.
smart_moves: The PR did not just flip the matching status in configure.py; it also updated obj_files.mk to make the actual linked object come from src, preventing the stale asm object from continuing to mask the C object.
Deleting the assembly fallback after enabling the C object made the decomp progress explicit and reduced the chance of future divergence between C and asm copies.
The change was minimal and targeted: only the assembly fallback and the two build-list entries changed, which is appropriate for a link re-enable PR when the source C already exists and matches.
decomp_lessons: When a module becomes matching, update both the project configuration classification and the concrete object list; in this PR that meant configure.py and obj_files.mk had to agree on src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c.o.
Removing the .s file is an important cleanup step after restoring C linkage because it prevents accidental fallback to assembly and makes remaining nonmatching modules easier to audit.
A build-system-only matching flip can still represent real decomp progress if the C file already exists and the PR changes linkage from asm to src.
For sysdolphin/baselib modules, matching state is represented as Object(Matching, "sysdolphin/baselib/name.c") in configure.py and as a src object path in obj_files.mk.
naming_conventions: Public GObj GX-link functions in the removed asm used names such as GObj_GXReorder, GObj_SetupGXLink, GObj_SetupGXLinkMax, and GObj_SetupGXLinkMaxSorted.
Still-unknown/internal functions in this module retained address-style names in the removed asm, including HSD_GObjGXLink_8039084C, HSD_GObjGXLink_80390908, and HSD_GObjGXLink_803909D8.
Global/static data labels in the removed assembly included HSD_GObj_804D7820, HSD_GObj_804D7824, HSD_GObjLibInitData, and gap_07_804085xx_data placeholders.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use Object(Matching, "sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c") in configure.py once the C implementation can replace the asm object.
Replace $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/.../*.s.o entries with $(BUILD_DIR)/src/.../*.c.o entries in obj_files.mk when re-enabling C linkage.
Delete the assembly source only after the C object is treated as matching and linked, ensuring the build no longer depends on the fallback.

The deleted asm preserves useful behavioral clues for nearby work: GX link index is stored at gobj offset 0x3, priority/order at offset 0x5, link-list pointers at offsets 0x10 and 0x14, and callback/user pointer-like data at offset 0x1c; this is evidence from the removed assembly, not new code added in the PR.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that switched Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching) and deleted matching asm fallbacks.
Find later work that renames HSD_GObjGXLink_8039084C, HSD_GObjGXLink_80390908, or HSD_GObjGXLink_803909D8.
Audit sysdolphin/baselib modules still linked from asm in obj_files.mk after gobjgxlink was converted.
Look for PRs affecting HSD_GObj_804D7820, HSD_GObj_804D7824, or GObj GX link list structure fields.
